en: Police in Pune rural have arrested Manthan Dinesh Vaidya (25) and three others for allegedly killing Sanjay Baburao Kadu-Deshmukh (40), a notorious criminal, as revenge for the murder of Manthan’s father 23 years ago. The incident occurred at Shindewadi near the old Katraj tunnel around 10:20 pm on July 11. Police recovered a country-made pistol and three live cartridges from one of the accused during their investigation. A court has remanded all four to police custody for further inquiry.

The Brihanmumbai Municipal Corporation (BMC) has proposed appointing a five-year panel of 29 technical consultants to oversee major infrastructure projects in Mumbai, aiming to improve planning and execution. The proposal, set for review by the civic standing committee this week, includes financial penalties for design errors exceeding 15% of project value. Consultants will be responsible for designing and supervising bridges, flyovers, and tunnels, with fees capped at Rs 3.5 crore for most projects. This move follows criticism over recent design flaws in infrastructure works like the Vikhroli railway overbridge and Gopal Krishna Gokhale bridge.

Ananya Joshi, who moved to the US in 2022 for her master's at Northwestern University, had to leave after her employer failed to sponsor her H-1B visa. After working in Germany since January 2025, she is reconsidering returning to the US due to uncertainty and now plans to start a career in India, either in Mumbai or Bangalore.

On Sunday afternoon, Saiyed Husain, a 19-year-old class 11 student from Kalkaji, Delhi, was stabbed to death near a park in B Block of DDA Flats after a quarrel with two or three individuals. Police received a call at 2:23 pm and found Husain injured; he later died at AIIMS Trauma Centre. The suspects are identified but not yet apprehended.

After reaching the World Cup quarterfinals, Norway jumped 12 spots to 19th. Spain, led by Luis de la Fuente, regained the top spot in FIFA rankings after defeating Argentina 1-0 in extra time on Sunday; Argentina dropped to second while France stayed third. Morocco reached a record-high sixth place. Other notable changes include Brazil at fifth and Belgium moving up to eighth, swapping with the Netherlands who fell to ninth. Germany dropped out of the top 10 to 12th. Tunisia suffered the biggest fall, down 12 places to 57th, while Egypt climbed five spots to 24th.

The United States, along with Israel and Lebanon, initiated a pilot zone in southern Lebanon where the Lebanese army will take over security from Israeli forces in three villages: Froun, Srifa, and Zawtar al-Gharbiya. This follows a June 26 agreement after six rounds of negotiations. However, Hezbollah opposes the deal, demanding unconditional Israeli withdrawal. Despite US statements, questions remain about the timing of Israeli troop withdrawals, with local mayors reporting no changes on the ground. Since March, over 4,300 Lebanese have been killed and more than a million displaced due to ongoing conflict involving rocket attacks by Hezbollah against Israel.

At least 25 people were killed as floods and landslides hit northern India following days of torrential monsoon rain. In Indian-administered Kashmir, at least 15 died in Poonch, Rajouri, and Doda districts; in Nagaland, nine died from multiple landslides. Rescue operations intensified with army personnel involved. Assam reported one death and over 57,000 affected. Authorities suspended pilgrimages due to safety concerns.

Morgan Freeman, known for his acting career, turned his 124-acre ranch in Mississippi into a bee sanctuary to address declining bee populations. In 2014, he imported beehives and planted bee-friendly vegetation without harvesting honey. His efforts highlight the importance of bees in pollination and food production, inspiring conservation actions globally.

In 1991, Raj Subramaniam, an Indian student in the US on a visa, joined FedEx after his roommate rejected an interview opportunity. Starting as an associate analyst, he rose through the ranks over 35 years to become CEO in 2022. Born and raised in India, Subramaniam studied engineering at IIT-Bombay before pursuing MBA in Texas. His journey highlights unexpected opportunities leading to leadership roles.

The Coral Reefs Rediscovering and Exploration project in Benin (COREB) rediscovered a deep-water coral reef off Benin's coast, previously thought dead since the 1960s. Using sonar and underwater drones, researchers found it thriving at over 50 meters depth, with eight coral types and fish species. This is the first confirmed mesophotic ecosystem in the Gulf of Guinea, suggesting more undiscovered reefs along West Africa's coast. The study was published in Frontiers in Marine Science.
